title: OpenAI's head of safety systems, Johannes Heidecke, is departing amid a reorganization that folds safety into research
reason: Failed publishing gate: wire-exclusive (policy/verification.md §2). Every outlet (Bloomberg, Engadget, Gizmodo, TechTimes, TheNextWeb, Technobezz, ThePrint, Investing.com, CryptoBriefing, AIWeekly) traces to one origin -- Wired's account of an internal memo -- despite broad relay. Core departure fact (a) has a Tier-0-adjacent element (OpenAI CRO Mark Chen gave Wired an on-record, named quote confirming Heidecke's departure), but that confirmation reached the public through only Wired, so it doesn't by itself clear the >=2-independent-source business-claim bar. The reorg texture -- safety reporting to a new 'VP of Research and Safety' Mia Glaese, Saachi Jain as interim head of safety systems -- is single-sourced to Wired's characterization of the memo, no independent outlet or OpenAI page confirms the specific titles/reporting lines. The 'sixth safety leader in two years' framing is a characterization/count (five priors: Jan Leike, Miles Brundage, Steven Adler, Andrea Vallone, Joshua Achiam -- each independently well-documented at the time) rather than our own verified tally, and 'safety-focused' is a categorization judgment for at least one name (Achiam, Chief Futurist/Mission Alignment).
watch: A second outlet independently reaching OpenAI or Heidecke (not relaying Wired's exclusive) with corroborating or conflicting detail
An OpenAI.com/careers-page, LinkedIn, or X update reflecting Glaese's 'VP of Research and Safety' title or Jain's interim role
Heidecke's own public statement on departure
cycle_2026-07-13_night2_c2_note: Opened this cycle from discovery-sweep-adjacent triage (raw ingest + web search), fresh verifier sub-agent run. Verdict: (a) departure fact confirmed (Tier-0-adjacent, on-record Mark Chen quote to Wired), (b) org-structure details (Glaese title, Jain interim role) single-source, (c) 'sixth leader' framing is reporting's characterization not our own tally. Wire-exclusive check applies -- does not clear §3's business-claim bar. Not published; opened as watching rather than dropped because the underlying departure is well-attested and a second independent source is plausible within days (departure reportedly effective by 2026-07-24).
cycle_2026-07-13_night2_c5_note: Triggered by fresh ingest: Business Insider's 'The leaders responsible for keeping OpenAI's AI safe keep leaving' (published 2026-07-12T20:24Z) looked like a candidate independent second source. Checked via 2 web searches -- it is a wider trend/pattern piece (folding in Joshua Achiam's separate Mission Alignment-team departure) but for the Heidecke fact specifically it still relays the same Mark Chen quote given to Wired, not independent reporting. No new outlet has reached OpenAI/Heidecke independently of Wired's exclusive. Gate still fails on wire-exclusivity. Still watching.

No OpenAI.com/careers or LinkedIn update on Glaese/Jain titles found. No Heidecke public statement found. Gate still fails on wire-exclusivity. Still watching.
